widget.title: Widget Configuration
widget.subtitle: Customize the virtual try-on widget for your store
widget.linkTextHelp: Label shown on the try-on link or button on product pages
widget.embedPositionBelow: Below buy buttons (add to cart / dynamic checkout)
widget.embedPositionHelp: Where the try-on appears relative to your theme’s purchase block.
widget.ctaTypeHelp: For the button style, upload your store logo for the best branded look. Without a logo, the button shows text only.
widget.tryonLayoutSidebar: Sidebar — branded left panel with progress
widget.tryonLayoutHelp: Classic keeps the current full-width flow. Sidebar adds a branded left panel with step progress.
widget.storeLogoHelp: Upload your store logo to appear in the widget. Accepted formats: JPG, PNG, GIF. Max size: 2MB.
widget.primaryColorHelp: Color used for the link and widget highlights
widget.fontHint: The widget automatically uses your store font for visual consistency.
widget.installationHelp: The widget is installed automatically. The "Try virtual fit" link will appear below the add to cart button on all product pages.
widget.installationHelpDynamic: The widget is injected on product pages. Position (above or below buy buttons) and style (link or logo button) follow what you save here.
widget.installationHelp2: Make sure the app is enabled in your theme settings.
widget.importantBullet1: The "Try virtual fit" link always appears below the add to cart button
widget.importantBullet1Dynamic: Storefront position and style (link or button) follow your settings above
widget.importantBullet2: The widget font is inherited from your store
widget.importantBullet3: Customizations are applied automatically on all product pages
widget.importantBullet4: Changes may take a few minutes to appear in the store
widget.errorSupabase: Supabase not configured. Check VITE_SUPABASE_URL and VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY in your environment.
widget.errorUploadLogo: Error uploading logo. Please try again.
widget.errorSaveConfig: Error saving configuration. Please try again.
widget.errorRlsWidgetConfigurations: Supabase permission denied (RLS) when saving widget configuration. Run SQL: supabase_fix_widget_configurations_rls.sql
widget.errorInvalidImageFile: Please select a valid image file.
widget.warnExcludedCollectionsColumnMissing: Excluded collections were not saved because the "excluded_collections" column does not exist in the database yet.
widget.excludedCollectionsTitle: Collections where the widget should NOT appear
widget.excludedCollectionsHelp: Select collections to hide the Omafit widget on those product pages.

dashboard.title: Omafit Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Welcome to Omafit virtual try-on
dashboard.extraImagesBanner: You are using {count} try-on session(s) above your included limit. Additional cost: {currency} {price} per try-on session.
dashboard.noPlanMessage: You don't have an active plan yet. Choose a plan to start using Omafit.
dashboard.aboutDescription: Omafit is an AI-powered virtual try-on that lets your customers try your clothes virtually, increasing purchase confidence and reducing returns.
dashboard.bestPracticesTitle: Tips to get the best from Omafit
dashboard.bestPracticesSubtitle: Best practices to improve size recommendations, try-on quality, and conversion.
dashboard.bestPracticeImages: • Use front-facing product photos with good lighting, high resolution, and clean background.
dashboard.bestPracticeVariants: • Keep product variants (color, size, and availability) well defined and up to date.
dashboard.bestPracticeCollections: • Organize collections with clear, consistent handles to improve rules and size chart mapping.
dashboard.bestPracticeSizeCharts: • Configure size charts by collection and gender using real measurements from your products.
dashboard.bestPracticeCatalog: • Avoid duplicate titles and incomplete descriptions; standardized catalog data improves UX and analytics.

sizeChart.title: Size charts
sizeChart.subtitle: Configure charts by collection and gender (male, female, unisex). The widget uses collection and gender to pick the table.
sizeChart.configureHelp: Each collection can have 3 tables (male, female, unisex). The widget receives the collection handle and gender and fetches the matching table. Use the default collection for products outside a specific collection.
sizeChart.noSizes: No sizes. Add the first one.
sizeChart.tableDisabledHint: This table is disabled. Enable it to configure.
sizeChart.howBullet1: Configure one or more collections (handle must match Shopify). Use the default collection for the entire store.
sizeChart.howBullet2: Per collection you have 3 tables: male, female, and unisex.
sizeChart.howBullet3: For apparel, choose exactly 3 measurement references. For footwear, the reference is fixed: foot size (cm).
sizeChart.howBullet4: The widget receives collection and gender and uses the matching table to suggest size.
sizeChart.errorRlsSizeCharts: Supabase permission denied (RLS) when saving size charts. Run SQL: supabase_fix_size_charts_rls.sql
sizeChart.warnCollectionMetadataNotPersisted: Tables were saved, but collection type/elasticity were not persisted yet. Run Supabase migrations.
sizeChart.collectionElasticityTitle: How does this collection's fabric behave on the body?
sizeChart.collectionElasticityHighElasticity: High elasticity (rib knit, very stretchable pieces)
sizeChart.videoSubtitle: Watch this video for a simple, business-focused walkthrough of applying size charts to your catalog.

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.subtitle: Processed try-on sessions and statistics by gender
analytics.errorShopDomain: Shop domain not found. Make sure you are accessing from Shopify Admin.
analytics.ordersError: Could not load orders and returns. Confirm the app has "Read orders" (read_orders) permission and the store has accepted permissions.
analytics.returnsHelp: Number of orders with returns in the selected period. "Before" = same number of days before app install; "After" = recent days (after Omafit).
analytics.conversionHelp: Percentage of orders that were not returned in the period. Higher is better.
analytics.byCollectionHelp: Most suggested size, preferred fit, and most chosen body type per collection.
analytics.noDataByCollection: No data by collection/gender in this period. Sessions are grouped by collection when the widget sends collection_handle when saving the session.
analytics.errorRlsAnalytics: Supabase permission denied (RLS) for analytics tables. Run SQL: supabase_fix_analytics_rls.sql
analytics.financialImpactSubtitle: Executive summary: estimated ROI, Omafit-attributed revenue, and estimated avoided cost.
analytics.financialRoiHelp: Based on attributed revenue and estimated plan/period cost.
analytics.financialRevenueHelp: Orders with at least one line item marked as _source=omafit_tryon.
analytics.financialAvoidedCostHelp: Estimated from return reduction and attributed average ticket.
analytics.performanceSubtitle: Engagement funnel, session time, and usage rate by category/collection.
analytics.performanceAvgSessionHelp: Average between session start and end timestamps.
analytics.performanceNoSessionData: No session data in this period.
analytics.qualitySubtitle: Returns before/after, users vs non-users comparison, and size-chart divergence alerts.
analytics.qualityUsersVsNonUsersHelp: Share of Omafit-attributed orders over total orders.
analytics.qualityNoSessionCoverage: No session data in this period to evaluate divergence.
analytics.qualityLowCoverage: Low recommendation coverage ({percent}%). Review collection size charts and size variants.
analytics.intelligenceSubtitle: Body-shape distribution, most recommended sizes, SKU/collection heatmap, and gender averages.
analytics.intelligenceNoBodyData: No body-shape data in this period.
analytics.intelligenceNoSizeData: No size data in this period.

billing.title: Billing
billing.plansSubtitle: Choose the right plan for your store. Billing is through Shopify.
billing.planOndemandDesc: 50 free try-on sessions (one-time, when account is created). Then $0.18 per try-on session.
billing.planProDesc: 3,000 try-on sessions included. Ideal for stores with higher volume.
billing.planGrowthDesc: 700 try-on sessions included per month. Then $0.12 per additional session.
billing.planEnterpriseDesc: Unlimited try-on sessions. One flat monthly fee for high volume.
billing.usageImagesUsedOnDemand: {used} try-on sessions generated – pay per use
billing.usageOverLimit: ⚠️ You have exceeded the included limit
billing.usageOverLimitNote: Extra try-on sessions will be charged automatically according to your plan.
billing.usageNearLimit: 💡 You are close to the limit
billing.usageNearLimitNote: Consider upgrading your plan if you need more try-on sessions.
billing.extraImagesDescription: {count} extra try-on session generated this month. Estimated cost: ${price}
billing.extraImagesDescriptionPlural: {count} extra try-on sessions generated this month. Estimated cost: ${price}
billing.wantToChangePlan: I want to change my plan
billing.errorSyncBillingWithShopify: Failed to sync billing with Shopify.

arEyewear.title: AR Accessories
arEyewear.subtitle: Create and publish 3D models of your accessories (e.g. eyewear) for a more realistic virtual try-on.
arEyewear.uploadHelp: One clear product photo (a light background usually works best). Optional extra photos on advanced manual upload.
arEyewear.variantSelectHelp: Each request is tied to the variant you pick (for example a colour). For several options in the try-on, create one request per variant — publishing one does not remove models already live for the others.
arEyewear.errorVariantRequired: Choose which product variant (colour, size, etc.) this virtual model represents.
arEyewear.publishMetafieldExplain: When you publish to the store, the main virtual model is always linked to the product so the try-on appears on the page. If you picked a variant, we also update that option so shoppers see the right model when they switch colour or size. Several options? Create one request and publish once per variant.
arEyewear.publishDoneProductOnly: Done: the model is live on the product. This request had no specific variant — every option shares the same model until you add per-variant requests.
arEyewear.publishDoneProductAndVariant: Done: the model is live on the product and on the variant you selected.
arEyewear.publishDoneVariantFailed: The model is live on the product, but we could not link it to the variant. Check your store settings or Omafit support — switching variants in the try-on may not update the model.
arEyewear.errorSelectImage: Choose the photo we will use to build the virtual model.
arEyewear.warnShop: Open from the embedded Shopify admin for shop context.
arEyewear.shopProductsHelp: Pick the product and the photo where the accessory looks best to shoppers — we use it to create the try-on model.
arEyewear.shopProductsEmpty: No products matched eyewear heuristics. Check product type, Shopify product category, or tags (e.g. glasses, sunglasses).
arEyewear.shopImagesHelp: Prefer a front-facing, sharp, well-lit photo where the product is easy to see.
arEyewear.whiteBackgroundHint: Tip: a light or neutral background usually looks cleaner and more premium in the try-on.
arEyewear.selectGenerationImage: Photo to build the virtual model
arEyewear.confirmShopImages: Confirm images and send to processing
arEyewear.needThreeDistinct: Pick three different images (or use manual upload below).
arEyewear.needThreeImages: This product has fewer than 3 images in the store. Add photos in Shopify or use manual upload.
arEyewear.needOneImage: This product has no images in the store. Add at least one photo in Shopify.
arEyewear.queueWorkerBanner: We are preparing one or more models. Refresh in a moment for the latest status.

detection.hint: We use tags first (e.g. ar:watch), then the Shopify product category (e.g. … > Watches), product type, and title. To force a type, add ar:watch, ar:bracelet, ar:necklace or ar:glasses before uploading.

calibrate.title: Polish how your try-on looks to shoppers
calibrate.subtitle: We show the accessory on a simple reference silhouette. Adjust the controls until it feels natural — the way a customer would expect it to sit in your store experience.
calibrate.howToIntro: Each control moves or turns the piece in the preview. Aim for a comfortable, believable fit on the face, wrist, or neck — whichever matches your product.
calibrate.howToStep1: Start with "Tilt up/down" and "Turn left/right" until the piece faces forward, with lenses lined up to the reference eyes for eyewear.
calibrate.howToStep2: Use "Height" to raise or lower — temples should sit just above the ear line on the silhouette.
calibrate.howToStep3: For finishing touches, use "Move closer/further" and the sideways nudge controls.
calibrate.howToStep4: When you like what you see, click "Save this look". Visitors will see that placement in your online try-on.
calibrate.previewHint: For glasses, align the piece with the eyes on the silhouette — that reference keeps results consistent.
calibrate.targetHelp: Choose "Whole product" when every colour or size should share the same placement. Choose "Only this variant" when each option needs its own fine-tuning.
calibrate.saveSuccess: Saved to your store. Open the product page and refresh to see it in the try-on — browsers sometimes show a cached page first.
calibrate.tip1: If the piece looks sideways, use "Turn left/right" until it faces the shopper.
calibrate.tip2: If it looks upside down, correct it with "Tilt up/down" or "Roll sideways".
calibrate.tip3: If the accessory feels too big or small, adjust "Size" before changing height.
calibrate.previewErrorNoScene: We could not show this model in the preview — the file may be incomplete. Try publishing the model again from AR Accessories.
calibrate.previewErrorNoGeometry: We could not find visible shapes in this file for the preview. Check the file or regenerate the model.
calibrate.previewErrorProcess: We could not prepare the preview. More information: {detail}
calibrate.previewErrorLoadModel: We could not load the model for preview. Detail: {detail}
calibrate.previewErrorEngine: The preview could not start in this browser. Detail: {detail}

tilt.label: Tilt up / down
tilt.help: Use when the piece looks tipped toward or away from the viewer.

helpByType.watch: When the watch looks tipped forward or backward on the wrist.
helpByType.bracelet: When the bracelet looks tipped forward or backward.
helpByType.necklace: When the necklace looks tipped forward or backward on the neck.

yaw.label: Turn left / right
yaw.help: Use when the piece shows its edge instead of facing the shopper.

helpByType.watch: When the dial is sideways instead of facing the camera.
helpByType.bracelet: When the bracelet twists instead of following the wrist.

roll.label: Roll sideways
roll.help: Straightens the piece when it looks cocked to one side.

helpByType.watch: Rolls along the arm — one edge lifts, the other lowers.
helpByType.bracelet: Rolls along the arm — one edge lifts, the other lowers.

height.label: Height on face
height.help: Raise or lower the glasses to align with the eyes.

depth.label: Move closer / further from the face

labelByType.watch: Move closer / further from the wrist
labelByType.bracelet: Move closer / further from the wrist
labelByType.necklace: Move closer / further from the neck

depth.help: Brings the piece nearer to the face or sets it slightly farther back.

helpByType.watch: Brings the watch closer to the skin or lifts it slightly off the wrist.
helpByType.bracelet: Brings the bracelet closer to the skin or lifts it slightly.
helpByType.necklace: Brings the necklace closer to the neck or adds a little space.

nudgeY.label: Height on face
nudgeY.help: Moves the glasses up or down (in centimetres) to match the eyeline.

helpByType.watch: Slides along the arm — handy if the watch sits too high or low on the wrist.
helpByType.bracelet: Slides along the arm to match where a customer would wear it.
helpByType.necklace: Raises or lowers until the pendant sits where you want it.

nudgeX.label: Nudge sideways
nudgeX.help: Centres the piece if it drifts left or right.

helpByType.watch: Shifts the watch left or right on the wrist.
helpByType.bracelet: Shifts the bracelet left or right on the wrist.
helpByType.necklace: Shifts the necklace left or right.

scale.label: Size
scale.help: Scales the whole piece. Go gently — too large can cover the face.

helpByType.watch: Scales the watch — very small sizes disappear; very large sizes look less believable.
helpByType.bracelet: Scales the bracelet — very large values can look heavy on the wrist.
helpByType.necklace: Scales the necklace — very large values can dominate the neckline.
